tagline: Set up your private AI in a few clicks.
agent_intro: Pick an AI provider, paste your API key, and connect. Your key is stored only on this VM and is never sent to CloudHosting.
builder_intro: This is a visual builder. Open it and configure your AI provider inside its own interface. The panel does not store your provider key for builder products.
model_ph: leave blank to use the suggested model
save_ok: Key validated and saved. The agent is being reloaded.
save_fail_validate: Could not validate the key against the provider. It was NOT saved. Check the key and try again.
save_fail_write: The key was valid but the config could not be written. Tell your provider.
open_product_hint: Opens your {product} workspace in a new tab.
telegram_intro: Your agent talks to you through Telegram. Create a bot once and paste its token below.
telegram_step_2: Send /newbot and follow the prompts (name + username ending in 'bot').
telegram_step_3: BotFather replies with a token like 123456:ABC-DEF... Copy it.
telegram_step_4: Paste the token below and save. Then message your new bot.
telegram_step_5: Send /start, then ask the bot @userinfobot for your numeric user id and paste it as the allowed user.
manual_generic: Your AI runs on your own VM. Your data and API key stay on this machine.
You pay the provider directly for usage; CloudHosting only runs the VM.
If replies stop working, re-check the key status above and confirm your provider has balance.
manual_hermes: Hermes is a personal agent that lives in Telegram and learns from your chats.
After connecting a key and a Telegram bot, message the bot from your allowed account.
It can run scheduled tasks and remembers past conversations on this VM.
manual_openclaw: OpenClaw is an agent that can run commands and code on this VM to do real work.
It answers only your allowed Telegram account; keep that lock in place.
Connect a key and a Telegram bot, then DM the bot to give it tasks.
manual_flowise: Flowise is a drag-and-drop builder for chat flows and agents.
Inside Flowise, add a ChatOpenAI node, set its Base Path to the provider endpoint and attach your key credential.
Type a model id served by your provider, e.g. anthropic/claude-opus-4.8.
manual_langflow: Langflow is a visual editor for agents and RAG pipelines.
Drop a Language Model / OpenAI component into a flow and point it at your provider endpoint.
Your avots key is preloaded as a Credential global variable if provisioned.
manual_dify: Dify is a platform for chatbots, RAG apps and workflows.
Go to Settings -> Model Provider -> OpenAI-API-Compatible and add a model.
Set Function calling to 'Tool Call', or agents and workflows cannot use tools.
footer: Powered by CloudHosting. Your AI, your VM, your data.
missing_product: This panel has no PRODUCT configured. Set the PRODUCT env var.
